answer:
\boxed{C}

A lateral sliding flap on a tooth with long-standing root denudation (6 years) will likely reveal **epithelial attachment** (e.g., junctional epithelium) at the previously exposed root surface due to periodontal breakdown and epithelial migration. The periodontal ligament (PDL) fibers are lost, and dentin is no longer directly exposed to the oral environment. This is a hallmark of chronic gingival recession or periodontal disease.
